title: Only Touch the Hem
subtitle: The bleeding woman reaches through the crowd for the edge of His garment

stillness: Be still, and draw near in secret.
She came up behind Him, from behind, ashamed of her affliction.
Let your whole intention be gathered, as hers was, upon one thing.
gospel: And while Jesus went on, behold, a woman, troubled with an issue of blood,
who for twelve years had been in a flux of blood,
who had spent all her substance upon physicians, and uselessly,
came up behind, and from behind,
and touched the hem of His garment.
For she said within herself, believing firmly:
If I shall touch only His garment, I shall be healed of my infirmity.
And touching, at once she was made whole of her plague.
And Jesus, knowing in Himself the power that had gone out from Him, said:
And the woman, seeing that she was not hidden,
fearing in heart and trembling in body before the majesty of His divinity,
came and fell down before His feet,
and declared before all the people the cause of her touching, and her healing.
And Jesus, turning and seeing her, said to her:
Be of good heart, daughter; thy faith hath made thee whole.
behold: Behold: the Lord had no doorkeepers, no guards, no servants, none to beat back the press of the crowd.
And so the woman draws near freely, while the crowds throng and press against Him.
See how she comes up behind, and from behind —
ashamed of her unclean and foul infirmity,
unable to come before His face because of the throng.
Mark her humility: she judged herself unworthy to touch the Lord's feet, or the fullness of His garment,
and so she touched only the hem.
She touched it not by bodily contact only, but by the devotion of faith.
Behold her, seeing she was not hidden:
fearing in heart and trembling in body before the majesty of that divinity whose power she had felt within herself,
she falls down before His feet, and confesses the truth before all the people.
consider: Despairing of the health the physicians could give, on whom she had poured out all she had,
she understood that the heavenly Physician was at hand, and placed her whole intention in Him.
She received her medicine out of His wound — and woe to those for whom the very medicine is turned into a wound!
She came, and coming she spoke, and speaking she touched, and touching she received her cure;
for by these three — by word, by faith, and by deed — is all salvation acquired.
He asks Who hath touched me? — not out of ignorance, that He might be taught,
but that the healed woman might confess, and through confession her faith might appear, and be commended by Christ.
The crowds pressed and touched Him bodily in that crush,
yet they did not touch Him with such devotion and faith as this woman had touched Him;
and it was of such a touch that the Lord was singularly asking.
He did not say, I have healed thee, but, Thy faith hath made thee whole —
teaching us to shun all display, and to exalt the merit of faith.
In our virtuous acts we must seek and proclaim not our own praise, but God's.
He called her daughter — for faith in Christ bestows the adoption of God's children.
Go in peace, He said — thou who before wast in turmoil, through thy long bodily infirmity:
for in peace God dwells; and she was healed not in body only, but changed from the causes of bodily passion, that is, from sins.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, I adore the feet of Thy mercy and Thy truth:
I beseech Thee, most loving One, heal by the touch of Thy grace my soul, bloodied and stained with manifold sins.
Raise it up from the death of an evil and hidden will and purpose,
and restore it to God Thy Father, to whom Thou hast adopted me as a son among Thy co-heirs.
Remember not, good Lord, Thy justice against Thy sinner,
nor Thy wrath against Thy guilty one;
but be mindful of Thy kindness toward Thy creature,
and of Thy mercy toward Thy wretched one, O Lord my God. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Si tetigero tantum vestimentum ejus, salva ab infirmitate mea ero.
latinJewel.english: If I shall touch only His garment, I shall be healed of my infirmity.
